step_by_step_thinking:
I concur that trismus (Option C) accurately represents a key feature of Ludwig's angina due to its characteristic bilateral submandibular edema causing difficulty opening the mouth. This symptom underscores both the rapid onset and severe nature of the condition as patients struggle to open their mouths fully, which can lead to airway compromise if not promptly treated.
